title: Neural — AI Content Studio
shortDescription: A complete, production-ready AI content generation platform — marketing site, full auth, and 8 working authenticated app pages.
fullDescription:
Neural is an end-to-end AI content studio built for modern content teams that need to ship faster without sacrificing voice. The product ships with a polished marketing site, full authentication flow, and an authenticated application with 8 live pages: dashboard with live analytics widgets, AI Generator workspace with streaming output, a templates library with search and category filters, projects view (grid + list), analytics with charts and funnels, team management with role-based permissions, settings with 7 sections, and billing with plan/usage/invoices.

The design system is a single source of truth — change once, update everywhere. The whole product is responsive, accessible, and ready to ship. No external API keys or backend required for the demo; everything is wired to land cleanly on top of a real LLM provider when you flip the switch.

I built this as a complete React + Vite + TypeScript + Tailwind application from scratch — routing, layouts, design system, page composition, and the visual polish that makes recruiters stop scrolling.
architecture: Vite SPA with React Router. Route groups separate marketing (landing/pricing/blog) from authenticated app. Atomic design tokens drive every component. Streaming output simulated via async generators.
lessonsLearned: The biggest lesson was treating the design system as code. Centralizing every color, spacing, and typography decision in one file eliminated 80% of UI drift and made 'ship a new page' mean 'compose existing primitives.'
challenges: Keeping the marketing site and the authenticated app visually coherent without code duplication. Solved with shared Layout primitives and a single design tokens file.
businessImpact: 17 product surfaces, 100% responsive, zero external data dependencies. Ready to plug behind any LLM provider and ship as a real product.

media.caption: The AI content studio for teams that ship.
Hero landing with feature highlights and live generation metrics
Authenticated dashboard with live analytics widgets and recent projects
AI Generator workspace with format/tone/length controls and live output streaming
Analytics with charts, funnels, and traffic sources

title: Olive & Atlas — Hospitality Operations Platform
shortDescription: One calm dashboard for every property in a hospitality group — with an AI copilot that knows the business.
fullDescription:
Olive & Atlas is a multi-property hospitality operations platform delivered for a four-property New York hospitality group. It isn't a generic template — every screen, every piece of mock data, and every line of microcopy is written for one client.

The platform ships with 18 live screens spanning auth, daily ops, and settings. The product is built on a strict Atomic Design hierarchy — Atoms, Molecules, Organisms, Layout — so every screen reads consistently and the design system stays maintainable as the product grows.

Olive, the in-product AI copilot, is grounded in the same data the rest of the app shows. She knows the four properties, their cuisines, their managers, their typical weeks, the active suppliers, the recent AI insights, the recent activity. Click Ask Olive in the top bar and you're talking to a copilot that has read the business — not a chatbot with a marketing prompt.

The design intentionally avoids the standard cyan/violet/magenta neon look because it competes with the warmth of hospitality. Instead it ships a terracotta + cream palette on warm graphite, with Fraunces serif for display, Inter for body, and JetBrains Mono for code. Looks like the front of house feels.
architecture: Next.js App Router with route groups (auth) and (dashboard). Atomic Design component hierarchy. Mock data in typed lib/mockData.ts ready to swap for real API hooks. Server-rendered shell, client-rendered dashboards.
lessonsLearned: Hospitality dashboards look more like magazines than SaaS. The terracotta/cream/warm-graphite palette was a deliberate rejection of generic dashboard neon — it makes the product feel like it belongs in the businesses it serves.
challenges: Designing for four properties without four versions. Built a strict design system with property-color slots that any screen can theme per-property without duplicating layouts.
businessImpact: 18 live screens, full Atomic Design system, AI copilot grounded in real data. Designed for one client, easy to re-theme for the next.

media.caption: One calm dashboard for every property in your group

title: TemariAi — AI Tutor for African Students
shortDescription: ተማሪ AI — Solves Math, Physics, and Chemistry problems step-by-step, with social learning built in.
fullDescription:
TemariAi (ተማሪ AI, meaning 'Student AI' in Amharic) is an educational platform that makes AI tutoring accessible to African students. The platform solves Math, Physics, Chemistry, and conceptual problems with step-by-step explanations, then gamifies the experience with XP, battles, quests, and achievements.

The backend is a FastAPI service with dedicated solvers per subject, vision AI for handwritten problem parsing via Gemini Vision, and Groq-powered explanation engines that turn raw solutions into student-friendly language. Supabase handles persistence, Firebase handles auth, and a native Android client ships for primary device reach.

The social layer is real: leaderboards, friends, groups, AI-driven quest generation, and battle-style problem challenges. Students aren't just solving problems — they're connecting, competing, and forming study groups.

Built end-to-end: FastAPI backend, AI orchestration layer, Supabase schemas, the backend tests, Docker compose, and the Android Kotlin client.
architecture: FastAPI backend with subject-specific solvers (chemistry_solver, math_solver, physics_solver) orchestrated by solver_orchestrator. AI services split into gemini_vision (image→problem), problem_classifier (route to subject), groq_explanation (raw→student-friendly). Supabase for persistence, Firebase for auth tokens, Android (Kotlin) for mobile client.
lessonsLearned: A student tutor that just gives answers is a calculator with extra steps. The real product is the explanation layer: turn a black-box solution into something a student can read, follow, and learn from.
challenges: Handwritten math notation is genuinely hard for vision models. Solved with a two-stage pipeline: Gemini Vision extracts a symbolic representation, then the math_solver evaluates it — never letting the LLM do arithmetic.
businessImpact: Math, Physics, and Chemistry tutors in one app. Free to use, built to scale, designed for the connectivity realities of African students (offline-friendly Android client).

media.caption: AI tutor solving math, physics, and chemistry step-by-step

title: OP Square Gateway — WooCommerce Plugin
shortDescription: Custom Square Web Payments checkout for OPeptides — page-scoped assets, AJAX coupons, minimal Square data payload.
fullDescription:
OP Square Gateway is a WordPress/WooCommerce payment gateway plugin that integrates Square Web Payments into the OPeptides checkout flow with surgical precision. Square only ever sees (amount, location_id, order_number, 'Online order') — no product names, SKUs, or peptide identifiers ever leave the server.

The plugin handles the boring parts that nobody else does right: page-scoped asset enqueuing so the Square SDK only loads on the checkout page, AJAX-driven coupon apply/remove with WooCommerce coupon engine, a CartFlows-compatible 'Pay with Credit Card' CTA, server-side CreatePayment calls, and signed webhook handlers for the asynchronous payment lifecycle.

I wrote this plugin from scratch in object-oriented PHP, with a singleton main class that loads the gateway class only when WooCommerce's payment gateways array is being built — never at boot. Self-healing cache logic clears stale payment-page caches from earlier buggy versions.

v1.1.11 ships to OPeptides production.
architecture: Singleton plugin class instantiated at plugins_loaded priority 99 (after WC). Gateway class loaded lazily inside register_gateway() right before the WC gateways array is built. WP AJAX handlers for coupon + payment flow. WC legacy webhook endpoint for async payment events.
lessonsLearned: The hardest part of any payment integration isn't the API call — it's the boot order. Load too early and you break when WC isn't active. Load too late and your gateway isn't registered. Solution: lazy-load the gateway class only inside the gateways array hook.
challenges: CartFlows uses a custom template that doesn't fire standard WC checkout hooks, so the standard 'Pay with...' CTA doesn't render. Solved with a hybrid approach: PHP hooks where WC works, JS injection where CartFlows owns the page.
businessImpact: Live in OPeptides production. Square only ever sees minimal order data, zero PII leakage. Plugin handles apply/remove coupons, server-side CreatePayment, signed webhooks.

media.caption: Custom Square Web Payments gateway for WooCommerce
